The [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]' 137th season saw them enter their eleventh [[Super League XIV|Super League]] campaign, as well as the [[2009 Challenge Cup]].

==Death of Leon Walker==
On 22 March 2009, Leon Walker, a reserve player, collapsed in the 63rd minute of a game against [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] Reserves at Maesteg rugby union ground. He was airlifted to Morriston Hospital, [[Swansea]] where he was pronounced dead upon arrival.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.crusadersrfl.com/2009seasonmatch7.htm |title=RFL Statement – Celtic Crusaders v Wakefield Trinity Wildcats |access-date=14 March 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090524193215/http://www.crusadersrfl.com/2009seasonmatch7.htm |archive-date=24 May 2009 |url-status=usurped |df=dmy-all }}</ref> He was 20 years old at the time of his death.<ref>[http://www.news.com.au/heraldsun/sport/nrl/story/0,27074,25227297-14823,00.html Wakefield player Leon Walker dies in Super League reserve game] ''Herald Sun'', 23 March 2009</ref> An inquest subsequently found that his death was the result of a rare undiagnosed heart defect, and the coroner ruled that he died of natural causes.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/wales/8374518.stm |title=Heart defect killed rugby player |website=BBC News |date=23 November 2009 |access-date=5 June 2026}}</ref>

Walker started playing rugby league for the amateur side, the Churwell Chiefs. Walker joined the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in 2006 and played for the club for three years. After his rookie season he was named the Salford Reds Junior Academy Player of the Year.<ref>[http://www.reds.co.uk/newsroom/news116_120906.html Player of the Year awards] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080701013153/http://www.reds.co.uk/newsroom/news116_120906.html |date=1 July 2008 }} ''SalfordReds.co.uk'', 12 September 2006</ref> In 2007 Walker represented both the Yorkshire and England U18 sides.

He joined the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in November 2008, joining their senior academy side.<ref>[http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/08/11/11/manual_131301.html Walker signs for Wakefield] ''Sporting Life''</ref><ref>[http://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/wakefield-sport/Wakefield-Wildcats-sign-Reds39-Leon.4681502.jp Wakefield Wildcats sign Reds' Leon Walker] ''Wakefield Express'', 11 November 2008</ref>

Walker attended [[The Morley Academy|Morley High School]] and was a scaffolder by trade,<ref name=timesonline>[http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/rugby_league/article5956545.ece Wakefield mourn the tragic loss of Leon Walker]{{dead link|date=September 2024|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} ''The Times'', 23 March 2009</ref> and was the cousin and team mate of [[Luke Blake]].<ref name="Funeral for rugby collapse player">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/england/bradford/7978259.stm |title=Funeral for rugby collapse player |website=BBC News |date=2 April 2009 |access-date=5 June 2026}}</ref>
